desc: I am a sleepy coder.
I commit in the morning.
I commit in the evening.
I pushed a commit with "dead" 2 times.
When I delete code, I delete a lot.
When I delete code, I delete a lot.
I did 2 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - da33629: Fix parse_rules duplicating every rule via self-extend (#26) - eb89cc0: Fix CIDR-expanded targets being silently dropped (#24)

desc: I did 6 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - fa2d109: Fix ArgumentGroup.Register unconditional write to LongNameToArgument (#22) - 7c5263d: Fix case-insensitive subparser lookup in ParseFrom (#21) - 2647ad2: Fix populateMaps duplicate registration and cross-call accumulation (#20) - 45ba2b0: Fix MapOfHttpHeadersArgument.ResetDefaultValue to discard previous keys (#19) - d39d835: Fix ListOfIntsArgument.ResetDefaultValue to replace instead of append (#18) - 9f2038c: Fix ListOfStringsArgument.ResetDefaultValue to replace instead of append (#17)

desc: I did 3 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - 9ca71d9: fix missing newlines between groups in usage using multiple groups, fixes #15 - ef3ecaf: Fixed double newline in usage when no arguments exists in default group and arguments are in other groups, fixes #14 - 093bcfc: Fixed no alphabetical order in help message printing subparser names, fixes #13

desc: I did 4 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - d2172f9: Fix close_smb_session leaking dead connection resources (#38) - fd51fee: Fix ConnectionPool holding lock across network I/O (#36) - 9d5a5cc: Fix parse_lm_nt_hashes dropping both hashes when both provided (#34) - 6f12a69: Fix canPassTheHash always returning True (#32)

desc: I did 7 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - f4c088e: Fix potential WHILE loop index underflow in executor The WHILE implementation decremented the parent frame index to re-execute the WHILE node after each body iteration. If the index was already 0 (e.g. due to a GOTO modifying execution state), this would underflow to -1 causing incorrect execution. Add a guard. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) - e6bee73: Fix lone trailing $ in interpolateVariables() resolving to "0" A trailing $ with no following identifier characters produced varName "$" which resolved to "0" via the default. Now a lone $ is emitted as a literal dollar sign instead. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) - 5c3ce6f: Fix lexer silently accepting unterminated string literals readStringLiteral() returned STRING_LITERAL even when the closing quote was missing, causing scripts with typos like TYPE "hello to silently parse as if the string were properly closed. Now returns UNKNOWN token type so the parser reports an error. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) - bbc411c: Fix unguarded m_scriptStopAction access in executionFinished lambda The executionFinished lambda accessed m_scriptStopAction via raw 'this' pointer while other objects in the same lambda used QPointer guards. If MainWindow is destroyed before the lambda fires, this is a use-after-free. Guard it with QPointer like the other members. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) - b29f9d0: Fix missing row bounds check in readScreenText() readScreenText() validated col via the loop condition but never checked row against buf->rows(). Out-of-bounds or negative row values would access invalid memory in buf->character(). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) - d072c7d: Fix missing scriptExecutor cleanup when closing a tab When a session tab is closed, macroRecorder was stopped and disconnected but scriptExecutor was not. A running script would continue firing timer callbacks against destroyed widgets, causing use-after-free crashes. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) - 56d8074: Fix null pointer dereference in checkExpectCondition() screenBuffer() can return nullptr if the screen widget is destroyed mid-execution (e.g. tab closed while script runs). The CursorAtPos and CursorAtRow cases called screenBuffer()->cursorPosition() without a null check, causing a crash. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)

desc: I found the answer to the ultimate question of life, the universe, and everything!
My favorite word is "added".
body:
My favorite commit message words are:

1. added (used 2436 times)
2. kernel (used 1205 times)
3. update (used 800 times)
4. readme.md (used 663 times)
5. symbols (used 637 times)

desc: I am a polite coder.
body: I use words like "thanks" and "please" in my issues: - #16: [enhancement] Adding SMBv1 support - #38: [bug] setuptools CANNOT consider this value unless `scripts` is listed as `dynamic`. - #536: [bug] Could not make a working code example on connecting to LDAP with Kerberos from a linux machine to a Windows Server

desc: I pushed a commit with "cafe" 6 times.
I made an epic commit with a message over 500 chars.
I committed on the Halloween! Boo!

I've starred 111 my own repositories.
I committed on Friday the 13th, One… By One…
I commit at midnight.
I merged a PR with failing checks
